title: Doubts on Asuka x Shinji Couple Theory
display_title: Doubts on Asuka x Shinji Couple Theory
wikitext:
[]: Doubts on Asuka x Shinji Couple Theory             Quote Post by pedropdea » Tue Oct 11, 2011 10:27 am Hello to all! First of all, sorry if this is a repeated topic. I haven't found it elsewhere but, assuming I'm very new to the forum, It might has been published somewhere... I have a question around Shinji and Asuka being actually considered a couple along the series: why have Shinji strangled Asuka twice in EoE? And, by the end of the movie, was he really trying to kill her? Well, in the Rebuild, they... SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Doubts on Asuka x Shinji Couple Theory             Quote Post by BiQ » Tue Oct 11, 2011 10:51 am pedropdea wrote: First of all, sorry if this is a repeated topic.

[]:             Quote Post by pedropdea » Tue Oct 11, 2011 12:29 pm Hahaha thanks for the hint!

[]: Re: Doubts on Asuka x Shinji Couple Theory             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Oct 11, 2011 1:10 pm pedropdea wrote: Well, in the Rebuild, they... SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Tue Oct 11, 2011 1:11 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 11, 2011 1:55 pm Rebuild definitely has more blatant A=>S than we've seen before with the cook-off & scar tallying; for all that it comes over at least as RxS as the manga in the round. @topic I still maintain that the as-aired series may have some teen experimentation played as high-jinks for comic relief, but otherwise treated getting the MC to consider loving himself as the object of the exercise.

[]: SIRP feels incomplete too. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Oct 11, 2011 3:07 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Rebuild definitely has more blatant A=>S than we've seen before with the cook-off & scar tallying;

[]: Re: SIRP feels incomplete too. B/             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 11, 2011 3:54 pm >wasn't played for laughs. >Shinji turning blue >Asuka reaching for the mouthwash In the overall scheme of things, the humour involved may be black, but it is definitely there.

[]: Re: SIRP feels incomplete too. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Oct 11, 2011 4:11 pm ^Alright, but none of the other instances involved this , and the gasp attached.

[]:             Quote Post by Fleetleader101 » Tue Oct 11, 2011 4:30 pm I think it is very ambiguous myself, but if you remember late Jr. High early High School I swear all my "relationships" were like that. heck even in college its still like that. Each person trying to protect themselves and denying that they feel anything for the other person. I think with Asuka sending Shinji mixed signals in combination with his self image problems he is so dense and slow to realize what Asuka is getting at. Plus the directors cut episodes really add more evidence to suggest that Asuka did feel something for Shinji. Then in addition in rebuild that continues to be more blatantly obvious. Toward the end of the series when Asuka is mind raped things start changing in the relationships of the characters. When Asuka is being attack Shinji pleads with his father to let him go and save her, but Gendo wont risk Unit-01 (and I think his son really). But, he does use the lance, which is forbidden for some reason, to save her. I think this decision was driven by his need to protect his son and his wife(unit 01) and was not as calculated as we may have previously thought. Especially when Feutski objects to it you can tell that there are several underlying motives. I also saw a minor parrallel in the ShinjixAsuka and GendoxYui relationship here. (I admit this part is a stretch) It seems like gendo is using the lance to try and spare his son the pain of losing someone he cares about as well. Remember that several characters like Hikari catch on to the fact that Asuka cares for Shinji and all of the adults saw them to arguing like a married couple about 20 times in the series. So it is not as if Gendo would be completely unaware of the relationship developing. Back to the point though. I think this is the first time that Shinji starts to accept his feelings toward Asuka because in the episodes after this he speaks about her several times when she is not featured, but inversely Asuka is hurt by the mind rape, mother issues, and the lack of attention from Shinji. So, she starts ignoring him and everyone. By EoE then Shinji is finally pursuing Asuka, but by that point she has turned her shoulder to him fearing rejection and pain again. lol its a simple case of when one person pursues another the other one turns off until that magic moment comes when they both accept where they are in the relationship. This moment in my opinion comes at the EoE when in the middle of their fight, choking scene in Instrumentality as well as on the beach, ends in he and her finally admitting their need for one another. But again all of this is very ambiguous, but I still feel like there is plenty of evidence to show the AsukaxShinji relationship in the series and it is just plain obvious in Rebuild. Also, I think the ReixShinji is more of a sister brother thing that is going on. Its borderline insest in some scenes.

[]:             Quote Post by pedropdea » Tue Oct 11, 2011 6:52 pm Fleetleader101 wrote: heck even in college its still like that. Each person trying to protect themselves and denying that they feel anything for the other person.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 11, 2011 7:17 pm "Relationship" is a funny word anyway. I have a relationship with my cat, in that I feed her and she keeps my feet warm at night. I don't think Asuka and Shinji ever had a strong romantic relationship. Shinji wanted positive attention from Father, missed Mother, and Asuka was looking for attention from Kaji at first, then later on Shinji, as if to prove that she was an adult. I agree with Tines that it was all very experimental, which is the most you would get from kids that age anyway, and the parts that were not played for laughs were played for desperation. (EoTV touches on the interactions the kids had throughout the series, and not in a very fun way until the "Alternate Universe" part.) And I forget what everyone here last thought about the first time Shinji strangled Asuka in EoE, whether or not it was all within Instrumentality or of it were a flashback to an actual event. My personal opinion on that changes constantly. I think the last time I saw it I reacted to it as if it were an actual flashback, just not a flashback to that coffee spill scene in Episode 24.

[]:             Quote Post by Fleetleader101 » Tue Oct 11, 2011 9:38 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: " I don't think Asuka and Shinji ever had a strong romantic relationship. Shinji wanted positive attention from Father, missed Mother, and Asuka was looking for attention from Kaji at first, then later on Shinji, as if to prove that she was an adult. I agree with Tines that it was all very experimental, which is the most you would get from kids that age anyway, and the parts that were not played for laughs were played for desperation. (EoTV touches on the interactions the kids had throughout the series, and not in a very fun way until the "Alternate Universe" part.) And I forget what everyone here last thought about the first time Shinji strangled Asuka in EoE, whether or not it was all within Instrumentality or of it were a flashback to an actual event. My personal opinion on that changes constantly. I think the last time I saw it I reacted to it as if it were an actual flashback, just not a flashback to that coffee spill scene in Episode 24.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Oct 14, 2011 7:50 am Mr. Tines wrote: I still maintain that the as-aired series may have some teen experimentation played as high-jinks for comic relief, but otherwise treated getting the MC to consider loving himself as the object of the exercise.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 14, 2011 12:52 pm Bagheera wrote: On the strangling: I don't see how it can be an actual flashback.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Oct 14, 2011 1:18 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Obviously not, as Asuka is alive at the start of EoE; however, without the DC material you are left wondering kick : kiss :: strangled : ???

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 14, 2011 1:54 pm It's easy enough to say that now, but the DC versions weren't part of the original English language DVD release. Which meant that when, not quite 8 years ago, I started watching the series, it went directly from as aired to EoE -- so, come the kitchen scene, I was definitely going "kick : kiss :: strangled : WTF???"

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Oct 14, 2011 2:18 pm It's even true for the Japanese audience, as the DCs weren't created until after EoE was in theaters. Anyone who missed the showing that included Death had no warning of the sudden shift in their relationship at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Oct 14, 2011 2:37 pm NemZ wrote: It's even true for the Japanese audience, as the DCs weren't created until after EoE was in theaters. Anyone who missed the showing that included Death had no warning of the sudden shift in their relationship at all.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Oct 14, 2011 4:13 pm As I understand it the theatrical releases were the same as the DVD releases... which is to say that the version that had the entire instrumentality portion of the film didn't include the portions of Death that were created to set it up, such as Asuka's flashbacks and the coffee scene. BTW, it might help to explain my take on things to tell you that my viewing order for eva was: 1) First 1/2 or so of 23, until friend realized I'd never seen Eva before 2) 1-26 (dub) ~skip about a year~ 3) EoE ~skip a few years~ 4) Death ~skip a few more~ 5) DC eps 6) 1-26 + movies (sub)

Article: Doubts on Asuka x Shinji Couple Theory
 night. I don't think Asuka and Shinji ever had a strong romantic relationship. Shinji wanted positive attention from Father, missed Mother, and Asuka was looking for attention from Kaji at first, then later on Shinji, as if to prove that she was an adult. I agree with Tines that it was all very experimental, which is the most you would get from kids that age anyway, and the parts that were not played for laughs were played for desperation. (EoTV touches on the interactions the kids had throughout the series, and not in a very fun way until the "Alternate Universe" part.) And I forget what everyone here last thought about the first time Shinji strangled Asuka in EoE, whether or not it was all within Instrumentality or of it were a flashback to an actual event. My personal opinion on that changes constantly. I think the last time I saw it I reacted to it as if it were an actual flashback, just not a flashback to that coffee spill scene in Episode 24.
